abandon.defEn: To leave someone or something behind permanently; to give up completely
abandon.exEn: He refused to abandon his dream despite repeated failures.
abandon.exKo: 그는 반복된 실패에도 꿈을 포기하지 않았다.

abase.defEn: To lower someone in rank, dignity, or self-respect; to humiliate
abase.exEn: The manager’s insults were meant to abase the intern.
abase.exKo: 그 관리자의 모욕은 인턴을 비하하려는 의도였다.

abate.defEn: To become less intense or widespread; to reduce
abate.exEn: The storm finally began to abate after midnight.
abate.exKo: 폭풍은 자정이 지나서야 마침내 잦아들기 시작했다.

abbreviate.defEn: To shorten a word, phrase, or text

abdicate.defEn: To give up a position of power or responsibility, especially a throne
abdicate.exEn: The king decided to abdicate in favor of his daughter.
abdicate.exKo: 왕은 딸에게 왕위를 물려주기 위해 퇴위하기로 결정했다.

abduct.defEn: To take someone away illegally by force or deception
abduct.defKo: 불법적으로 강제로 또는 속여 데려가다; 유괴하다
abduct.exEn: The police launched a search after a child was abducted near the park.
abduct.exKo: 아이가 공원 근처에서 유괴된 후 경찰이 수색을 시작했다.

aberrant.defEn: Different from what is normal or expected; abnormal
aberrant.exEn: The scientist excluded the aberrant data point from the final analysis.
aberrant.exKo: 과학자는 최종 분석에서 비정상적인 데이터 값을 제외했다.

abet.defEn: To encourage or help someone do something wrong
abet.exEn: Anyone who abets fraud can be prosecuted.
abet.exKo: 사기를 부추기거나 돕는 사람은 누구나 기소될 수 있다.

abject.defEn: Extremely bad, miserable, or hopeless; showing no pride or dignity
abject.exEn: They lived in abject poverty for many years.
abject.exKo: 그들은 여러 해 동안 극심한 빈곤 속에서 살았다.

abjure.defEn: To formally renounce or reject a belief, claim, or allegiance
abjure.exEn: He was forced to abjure his political views in public.
abjure.exKo: 그는 공개적으로 자신의 정치적 견해를 철회하도록 강요받았다.

abolish.defEn: To officially end a law, system, or practice
abolish.defKo: 법, 제도, 관행 등을 공식적으로 폐지하다
abolish.exEn: The government voted to abolish the outdated regulation.

abominate.exEn: He abominates dishonesty in any form.
abominate.exKo: 그는 어떤 형태의 부정직함도 몹시 증오한다.

aboriginal.defEn: Relating to the earliest known inhabitants of a region; indigenous
aboriginal.exEn: The museum displayed aboriginal tools and artwork.

abortion.defEn: The deliberate ending of a pregnancy
abortion.exEn: The debate over abortion laws remains highly controversial.
abortion.exKo: 낙태법을 둘러싼 논쟁은 여전히 매우 논란이 크다.

abrasive.defEn: Capable of scraping or wearing something away; also harsh in manner
abrasive.exEn: His abrasive comments upset everyone at the meeting.
abrasive.exKo: 그의 거친 발언은 회의에 있던 모두를 불쾌하게 했다.

abridge.defEn: To shorten a text while keeping the essential meaning
abridge.exEn: The publisher decided to abridge the novel for young readers.
abridge.exKo: 출판사는 청소년 독자를 위해 그 소설을 축약하기로 했다.

abrogate.defEn: To formally repeal or do away with a law, agreement, or right
abrogate.exEn: The regime tried to abrogate the constitution overnight.
abrogate.exKo: 그 정권은 하룻밤 사이에 헌법을 폐지하려고 했다.

abrupt.defEn: Sudden and unexpected; also rude or short in speech
abrupt.exEn: The car made an abrupt stop at the red light.

abscission.defEn: The act or process of cutting off or separating, especially a natural shedding in plants
abscission.defKo: 절단·분리의 행위나 과정; 특히 식물의 자연 탈리
abscission.exEn: Drought can trigger leaf abscission in many trees.
abscission.exKo: 가뭄은 많은 나무에서 잎의 탈리를 유발할 수 있다.

abscond.defEn: To leave quickly and secretly to avoid arrest or punishment
abscond.exEn: The suspect absconded before the trial began.

absolve.defEn: To free someone from blame, guilt, or responsibility
absolve.exEn: New evidence may absolve him of the crime.
absolve.exKo: 새로운 증거가 그를 그 범죄 혐의에서 벗어나게 할 수 있다.

abstain.defEn: To choose not to do something; to refrain, especially from voting or consuming something
abstain.exEn: Several members abstained from voting on the proposal.
abstain.exKo: 몇몇 구성원은 그 제안에 대한 표결에서 기권했다.

abstruse.defEn: Difficult to understand because it is complex or obscure
abstruse.exEn: The professor’s lecture on quantum theory was fascinating but abstruse.
abstruse.exKo: 양자 이론에 대한 교수의 강의는 흥미로웠지만 난해했다.

allay.defEn: To reduce or calm fear, worry, or pain
allay.exEn: The doctor’s explanation helped allay my concerns.
allay.exKo: 의사의 설명은 내 걱정을 덜어 주었다.

avert.defEn: To prevent something bad from happening; to turn away
avert.exEn: Quick action averted a major accident.
